{"key_event":"Second Reform Act and mass working-class mobilization","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened notably as trade unions, reform leagues, cheap print, voluntary associations, and the 1867-68 franchise expansion converted urban working-class organization into formal electoral leverage. State capacity rose only modestly: Victorian administration, policing, inspection, and security responses to Fenianism deepened reach, but laissez-faire/free-trade policy and reliance on local/voluntary institutions kept the state below overwhelming penetration.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":8.4,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.7}
